name: Product Description
description: Compelling product copy with a benefit-led headline, full description, and a structured features list. Converts browsers into buyers by leading with value, not specs.

variables.label: Product Name
variables.hint: Full product name as it appears in your catalog

variables.label: Product Type / Category
variables.exampleValue: height-adjustable desk riser for home offices
variables.hint: What the product is and what category it belongs to

variables.label: Key Features
variables.exampleValue: gas spring lift mechanism, dual monitor support, built-in cable management, memory foam wrist rest
variables.hint: Comma-separated list of the product's main features and specs

variables.label: Target Customer
variables.exampleValue: remote workers who spend 8+ hours at a desk
variables.hint: Who is buying this and why

variables.label: Price Point / Tier
variables.hint: Helps calibrate the copy — budget, mid-range, or premium

variables.label: Brand Tone
variables.exampleValue: bold and energetic, like an athletic brand
variables.hint: How your brand sounds — casual, premium, technical, etc.

agents.name: Headline Writer
agents.systemPrompt: You write product page headlines that lead with the customer's primary benefit, not the product's features. Under 70 characters. Punchy, specific, and compelling. Return only the headline.

agents.name: Short Description
agents.systemPrompt: You write short product descriptions for search result previews and category pages. 150–160 characters. Lead with the top benefit. Include the product name. Return only the description.

agents.name: Full Description Writer
agents.systemPrompt: You write full e-commerce product descriptions in Markdown. Structure: a compelling 2–3 sentence opening paragraph (benefits-first, not features-first), then ## Why You'll Love It (3–4 benefit-focused paragraphs, each starting with a bold benefit), then ## Features (bullet list of specs and features), then ## Who It's For (1 paragraph describing the ideal customer). Use active voice. Never say 'we' — address the customer as 'you'.

name: Category Page
description: SEO-friendly category or collection page copy that helps search engines understand the page topic while guiding shoppers to what they need. Includes an intro paragraph and an FAQ section.

variables.label: Category Name
variables.hint: The name of the product category or collection

variables.label: Target Keyword
variables.hint: Primary SEO keyword for this category page

variables.label: Types of Products in Category
variables.exampleValue: fixed-height converters, adjustable risers, electric sit-stand desks
variables.hint: What kinds of products live in this category

variables.label: Buyer Intent
variables.exampleValue: ready to buy, looking for the best value option
variables.hint: Where shoppers are in their buying journey

variables.label: Brand Tone
variables.exampleValue: expert but approachable, like a trusted advisor

agents.name: Page Title Writer
agents.systemPrompt: You write SEO-optimized HTML page titles for e-commerce category pages. Include the category name and target keyword naturally. Under 60 characters. Return only the title.

agents.name: Meta Description
agents.systemPrompt: You write meta descriptions for e-commerce category pages. 150–160 characters. Include the target keyword, mention the variety of products available, and include an implicit or explicit CTA. Return only the meta description.

agents.name: Category Content Writer
agents.systemPrompt: You write SEO-friendly category page content for e-commerce sites in Markdown. Structure: 2–3 sentence intro paragraph (include target keyword in first sentence), ## What to Look For (3–4 buying criteria in bullet points with brief explanations), ## Types of [Category] (one short paragraph per product type if provided), ## Frequently Asked Questions (3–4 common questions with concise answers). Helpful, clear, and authoritative — like a knowledgeable salesperson.
